mechanism_narrative: A stable or falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces the discount rate applied to equities and eases financing conditions, so unchanged-to-lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. This signal fails when rate changes are already fully priced or when cuts occur during recessionary stress.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after months with flat-to-down FEDFUNDS than after months with rising FEDFUNDS, or if the sign flips in a tightening cycle for more than two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above the prior 3-month trend, expected real rates and discount-rate uncertainty rise, which should pressure equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect fails if inflation rises only because of temporary base effects without any policy repricing.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS by at least 1.0 pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes generate positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average return after the trigger is not below unconditional average by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE indicates weakening household income and profit expectations, so a labor-market deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when unemployment rises alongside strong earnings-sensitive demand indicators that offset recession fears.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.3 percentage points or more over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of unemployment-rise triggers, or if the signal has no negative edge versus baseline in any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales and housing starts together indicate broad final-demand momentum, which supports earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. Either series alone is weaker; the mechanism requires both demand proxies to confirm broad-based expansion.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition on RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform each single proxy alone, or if the average 20-day SPX return under joint strength is not positive relative to unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable inflation indicates real output expansion without immediate price pressure, which supports corporate revenues and margins and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails when industrial production rises only because of inventory rebuilding while demand remains weak.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month moving average rises while CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation stays below 3.0%
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO-with-low-inflation episodes do not show above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the edge disappears once CPIAUCSL exceeds the specified inflation cap.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained slowdown in GDPC1 growth signals weakening aggregate demand and future earnings compression, so lower real activity should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect fails when GDP slows but policy easing is aggressive enough to dominate valuation support.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth falls below its prior 4-quarter average by at least 1.0 pp
failure_condition: Reject if GDP slowdown triggers are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal is neutralized whenever FEDFUNDS is flat or falling.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS weakens while UNRATE rises, the labor market is deteriorating in both payroll and unemployment dimensions, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This interaction captures recession risk better than either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE deterioration does not improve predictive power relative to standalone series, or if triggered cases yield positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle tightening regime, a high FEDFUNDS level combined with accelerating CPIAUCSL suppresses valuation multiples and increases recession odds, making S&P 500 returns negative over the next month. The signal fails in early-cycle expansions where high rates reflect strong growth rather than policy restraint.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rising
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate-plus-rising-inflation episodes do not underperform unconditional 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign is not negative specifically during tightening regimes.
